subject: UX & Research
title: How Netflix Organizes Content
description: A 60-second lesson on How Netflix Organizes Content.

hook.text: Netflix has over 75,000 micro-genres. Not just 'Comedy' — but 'Dark Scandinavian Comedies' and 'Mind-Bending Dramas.'

buildup.text: Netflix's IA uses algorithmic categorization. Human taggers label content with hundreds of attributes per title.

discovery.text: These micro-genres power personalized rows. Each user sees a different set of categories based on viewing history.

twist.text: The category labels themselves are generated from user behavior patterns, not just editorial judgment.

climax.text: Netflix doesn't have one information architecture. It has millions — one per user, generated algorithmically.

punchline.text: One structure per user. Netflix personalized IA at scale.

quiz.question: How many micro-genres does Netflix use to categorize content?
